title: hieracium caesioides subsp. aleuroclinium
intro: Hieracium caesioides subsp. aleuroclinium is a subspecies of the Hieracium caesioides plant, commonly known as a type of hawkweed, which is a flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium caesioides subsp. aleuroclinium include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
